content.summary: Learn how to create reusable Python extensions for COMPs in TouchDesigner. Extensions let you attach custom Python classes to components, providing clean APIs, encapsulated state, and reusable behavior that persists across frames and can be promoted as custom parameters.

sections.title: What Are Extensions?
sections.content:
Extensions are Python classes that attach to a COMP, giving it custom methods and properties. When you call `op('/project1/myComp').MyMethod()`, you're calling an extension method.

Extensions are the recommended way to organize complex Python logic in TouchDesigner. They provide:
- **Encapsulation**: State and methods live together
- **Persistence**: The class instance survives across frames
- **Promotion**: Methods can be called from anywhere via `op()` references
- **Clean API**: Better than loose scripts in Execute DATs

sections.title: Create the Extension Class
sections.content:
Create a **Base COMP** and inside it, create a **Text DAT** named `MyExtensionExt`. Write a Python class that inherits from nothing (plain class) and takes the COMP as its first argument in `__init__`.

The class name should match what you'll register in the COMP's extension settings.

sections.title: Register the Extension
sections.content:
On the Base COMP, go to the **Extensions** parameter page. In the `Extension Object 1` parameter, enter the path to your Text DAT and the class name in the format:

`op('./MyExtensionExt').module.MyExtensionExt(me)`

This tells TouchDesigner to instantiate the class and attach it to the COMP. After setting this, the COMP gains all the public methods and properties from your class.
sections.code:
# Register the extension on the Base COMP
base = op('/project1/base1')
# Set the extension parameter
base.par.extension1 = "op('./MyExtensionExt').module.MyExtensionExt(me)"
# Reinitialize extensions
base.par.reinitextensions.pulse()

sections.title: Call Extension Methods
sections.content:
Once registered, you can call extension methods from anywhere in your project using the `op()` function. Methods that start with a capital letter are automatically promoted to the COMP's interface.

You can call these from parameter expressions, Script CHOPs, Execute DATs, or other extensions.
sections.code:
# Call extension methods from anywhere
myComp = op('/project1/base1')

# Call a method
result = myComp.Increment()
print(f'Count is now: {result}')

# Access a property
print(f'Current count: {myComp.Count}')

# Reset
myComp.Reset()

sections.title: Add Custom Parameters
sections.content:
Extensions become more powerful when combined with **Custom Parameters**. Add custom parameters to your Base COMP (right-click > Customize Component) and reference them in your extension via `self.ownerComp.par`.

This lets users configure your extension through the familiar TouchDesigner parameter UI, and you can react to parameter changes using a **Parameter Execute DAT** inside the COMP.
